name: Alumni Services
description: The Alumni Services Committee serves to assist the Brooklyn Tech Alumni Foundation in the planning, preparation and execution of events. These events include Career Day, Recent Alumni Day, Internship Forum, and Homecoming. Members of the Alumni Services Committee need to regularly stop by the Alumni office during their lunch periods or free periods to ask if their assistance is needed. Members will also need to be present on the day of the events to help run it successfully; this can range from greeting alumni to showcasing Tech. Students will often have an opportunity to speak to alumni about their own Tech experiences and learn more about life after high school.

name: Club Team Council
description: The NHS Club Team Council Committee bridges the gap between the Student Government Organization and the National Honor Society. While the SGO CTC branch manages an active roster of clubs, the NHS committee supports it by providing logistical support for special occasions, such as the Club Fair, and by tracking the occurrences of club meetings throughout the year.

name: College Services
description: The College Services Committee aids in the execution of College Office functions. Members help out by volunteering at the College Office to major events like the College Fair. Mainly taking place after school, members assist in tasks that range from setting up college booths to guiding parents and college representatives to the event location. Being part of the behind-the-scenes of these functions, members have the opportunity to learn valuable information as the events and the staff in the office will guide the way on their college process journey. Members also assist in spreading information to the rest of the student body regarding the college process. The College Office values any input from members and is always looking for new ways to engage the student body!

name: Community Services
description: The Community Services Committee is oriented towards students with a passion for bettering their communities through projects that involve beautification, assisting those who are less fortunate, and celebrating the cultural diversity of their city. We will work with several nonprofit organizations to facilitate awareness movements, walks, cultural parades/festivals, park cleanups, and more. In addition, members can receive official volunteer papers from the various organizations we volunteer with. As we enter the 2024-2025 school year, we aim to provide both virtual and in-person events with a stronger emphasis on in-person ones. Moreover, members are always welcome to suggest events or organizations to volunteer with to the Committee Chairpeople.

name: Fundraising
description: The Fundraising Committee  helps support the NHS community through its events to collect funds that are needed to support the organization as well as create new opportunities for its members. This entails both soliciting donations at school events, like Parent Teacher Conferences and the High School Fair, and facilitating fundraising initiatives aimed at our supportive student body. Fundraising Committee members understand that being well-financed is the logistical backbone of NHS and aim to bring the organization the support that it needs and deserves. Our mission is to provide the funds needed to keep the events, spirit, and traditions of NHS alive.

name: Induction
description: The Induction Committee is dedicated to the planning and execution of two major NHS events: the Induction Ceremony (which welcomes new members) and the Installation Dinner (which celebrates a successful school year of NHS and welcomes the new executive board). The committee will work towards both events alongside other NHS executives, members, and school staff. Induction Committee members will have the opportunity to attend planning meetings for both events, create the design for the ceremony programs, greet ceremony guests, facilitate both events on the day they occur, and much more. Members in the Induction Committee will have countless opportunities to develop their leadership skills, as well as their planning and organizational skills in the process of planning both events. The Induction Committee will meet frequently during the planning of all its events. In addition, the Induction Committee will plan new events that will benefit the Tech community, bring the NHS Community closer together, and contribute to a greater cause.

name: Mentoring
description: The Mentoring Committee is dedicated to welcoming freshmen and guiding students across all grade levels throughout Tech and  fostering empowerment among them. Mentoring Committee  members aim to create a collaborative and supportive school community, guide students through an easier high school journey,  and work with both underclassmen and upperclassmen from the transition to Tech to applying to college! Members participate in events to promote Brooklyn Tech to the broader community of schools.

name: Outreach
description: The Outreach Committee aims to guide, inform, and connect current and prospective NHS members through multimedia operations. The Committee consists of Visual/Graphic Design and Journalism subcommittees. Members will be tasked with partaking in information sessions, writing articles for the NHS Newsletter, creating graphics for events, and documenting the illustrious character that our members embody. The NHS Newsletter aims to provide an interactive review of current events within the NHS community as a whole, and within its respective committees. The Outreach Committee welcomes collaboration with other student-led organizations and with other NHS Committees in order to promote NHS to all students.

name: Parent Services
description: The Parent Services Committee partakes in all events involving parents. Each service year consists of a myriad of events, including helping out with PA meetings and the annual Cultural Fair. Members collaborate with parents, students, and the rest of the school community to facilitate a holistic connection throughout the school.

name: School Environment Services
description: The School Environment Services Committee is dedicated to ensuring a clean and safe school atmosphere that is inclusive of the student body. The committee works with students and the school administration to update facilities and provide resources within the school community. In addition, the committee will work to host engaging activities as well as ensure that everyone feels accepted in the school community. The goals of the committee are to provide a platform where students can voice concerns about Brooklyn Tech’s upkeep and help mitigate those problems, to encourage greater overall environmental awareness, and to provide the necessary support for BTHS students to achieve their goals.

name: Tours
description: The Tours Committee’s main objective is to provide a comprehensive view of Brooklyn Tech to both students and parents. Members of the committee are expected to act as tour guides, explaining student life and all that is associated with attending  Tech while also sharing real experiences with their audience. Helping to ease the concerns of prospective students and their parents, members also contribute their knowledge and experiences toward resources designed to make the transition to Tech smoother. Members should also be ready to provide tours to a variety of audiences including international guests and middle schoolers.

name: Tutoring
description: The Tutoring Committee serves to provide in-person and online tutoring in any subjects that pose a challenge to students in Brooklyn Tech. We provide one-on-one tutoring sessions during both free periods and after school taught by committee members who are proficient in the subject of interest. Tutors can also reach out to peers in their classes in need of extra help and tutor them in their free time. Tutors can also opt to provide virtual tutoring to students from the Prescod Institute for Sport, Teamwork, and Education, also known as the PISTE Academy.
